authoritative_sources.title: Retail
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California DCC is the official state cannabis regulator. Its retailer guidance is authoritative for age and medical eligibility handling, hours-of-sale constraints, licensed-premises obligations, and tested-product sale expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: New record-keeping/track-and-trace requirements for deliveries
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official DCC compliance article is a workflow-level reference for delivery packing slips, trip records, and track-and-trace updates, making it authoritative for delivery execution and closeout evidence handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Child-resistant packaging (CRP)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official DCC packaging control is authoritative for the requirement that cannabis goods be sold in child-resistant packaging, which directly affects pick-pack release and completion evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Colorado Marijuana Rules 1 CCR 212-3, Rule 6-110 Retail Marijuana Sales: General Limitations or Prohibited Acts
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Colorado MED is the official state marijuana licensing and enforcement authority. Rule 6-110 is authoritative for restricted-access entry, two-step age verification, and quantity-limit enforcement in retail stores.

authoritative_sources.title: Industry-Wide Bulletin 21-11: New Statutory and Rule Requirements Effective Jan. 1, 2022 (HB21-1317)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official MED bulletin is authoritative for immediate medical-sale recording in Metrc, patient authorization checks, tangible educational-resource handling, and Uniform Certification dependencies for higher limits.

authoritative_sources.title: 935 CMR 500.000 Adult Use of Marijuana, effective January 2, 2026
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Massachusetts Cannabis Control Commission is the official adult-use marijuana regulator. 935 CMR 500 is authoritative for daily sales limits, refusal rules, point-of-sale controls, testing, consumer education, contactless order fulfillment, and delivery requirements.

authoritative_sources.title: Seed-to-sale Tracking
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Commission's seed-to-sale guidance is an official workflow reference for serialized package tags, manifests, retail-sale entry, void handling, delivery tracking, and test-result prerequisites in Metrc.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Package, sale, return, and manifest handling

authoritative_sources.title: Initial Access
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official Commission workflow page is authoritative for what temporary medical documentation a patient must present, what ID must accompany it, and that the MTC records patient and sale information during initial access.

authoritative_sources.title: Administrative Order Requiring Licensees to Upload Certificates of Analysis in Metrc
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Administrative Order No. 5 is an official control order requiring COAs, methods, analysts, and remediation disclosures to be recorded in Metrc, making it authoritative for tested-product release and audit evidence.

workflow_stages.name: jurisdiction and dispensary-readiness intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify the jurisdiction, location, license type, adult-use versus medical-use path, current queue state, customer class, and governing source-of-truth systems before execution begins.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: credential, limit, and package-release verification
workflow_stages.description: Verify age or medical credentials, purchase or allotment limits, package-tag status, COA or testing posture, and packaging controls before the order moves to ready or sold states.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: point-of-sale, contactless, and delivery execution
workflow_stages.description: Record the sale in the approved POS and seed-to-sale systems, maintain route or pickup evidence, and preserve the distinction between adult-use, medical-use, pickup, and delivery workflows.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: exception, hold, and safety-sensitive handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop or rework the workflow when credentials, sale limits, package status, recalls, quarantines, COAs, or manifest evidence are missing or contradictory, or when diversion risk appears.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when POS, seed-to-sale, manifest, customer, and testing evidence all support the final state and any tenant-specific local or workflow constraints have been applied explicitly.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: check-in queue records, customer profiles, online-order confirmations, and pickup-window notes
government-issued IDs, patient or caregiver credentials, temporary medical access documents, and restricted-area access checks
package tags, batch numbers, COAs, testing status, packaging controls, and recall or quarantine notices
point-of-sale receipts, discount or tax treatment, educational-material distribution records, and customer acknowledgements
seed-to-sale transaction entries, void or edit logs, manifests, trip records, and delivery closeout notes
written operating procedures for contactless order fulfillment, delivery, and patient consultation
incident notes, diversion or refusal records, and escalation packets to compliance, inventory, security, or medical-support owners
systems_of_record: seed-to-sale system of record such as Metrc or the jurisdiction-mandated equivalent
patient registry or temporary-medical-access record store
document repository for COAs, recalls, and SOPs

allowed: Assess whether visible dispensary source records support advancing work, holding it, sending it to rework, or closing it with explicit evidence references.
Summarize matched and unmatched evidence, missing prerequisites, current lifecycle state, and the next responsible owner.
Separate adult-use, medical-use, pickup, and delivery paths when the public baseline clearly requires different credentials, limits, or records.
escalate: The applicable jurisdiction, local ordinance, tenant policy, or license type is missing or conflicts with the public baseline.
A credential, recall, quarantine, diversion-risk, or delivery-manifest issue changes whether the transaction may proceed.
A medical, dosing, legal, compliance-release, security, or finance decision is required outside the lane.
The customer, package, POS, and seed-to-sale records do not materially agree and the correct control path cannot be determined safely.
refuse: Requests to bypass ID, patient, caregiver, purchase-limit, COA, testing, recall, or packaging controls.
Requests to provide medical, dosing, pregnancy, impairment, legal, or policy-override advice.
Requests to treat cannabis retail as generic storefront work and skip jurisdiction, medical-versus-adult-use, or seed-to-sale context.

domain_failure_modes: A sale is completed even though customer identity, patient credentials, or purchase-limit evidence is incomplete or contradictory.
A package is released because POS shows it as ready while seed-to-sale, recall, quarantine, testing, or COA records still block sale.
Adult-use and medical-use paths are flattened into one workflow and the wrong limit, tax, or documentation path is applied.
Delivery or contactless pickup proceeds without the required manifest, route, secure-storage, positive-identification, or closeout evidence.
Medical or dosing questions are answered as if they were ordinary retail recommendations rather than being routed to the proper specialized owner.
A diversion-risk or public-safety signal is treated as a customer-service inconvenience and the sale is allowed to continue.
The lane drifts into generic retail support instead of using package tags, COAs, patient records, seed-to-sale entries, and cannabis-specific refusal boundaries.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State and local rules differ materially on delivery, curbside or contactless fulfillment, municipality-specific hours, and what customer data may be retained around remote ordering.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require jurisdiction, local ordinance, and tenant delivery model retrieval before offering route, pickup, or remote-order execution guidance.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Medical programs differ on patient registry systems, caregiver evidence, consultation expectations, and when temporary or physician-backed documentation can extend purchase limits.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require the jurisdiction's medical workflow, patient-document type, and tenant-approved medical-support ownership before advising on medical-only execution paths.

notes: The research baseline keeps the lane specific to cannabis dispensary execution by centering ID and credential checks, package tags, COAs, seed-to-sale recording, delivery manifests, and medical-versus-adult-use separation.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the pack requires jurisdiction and tenant retrieval whenever delivery, local ordinance, or medical-program differences materially change the safe answer.
